summarization.a: The figure displays infrared absorption spectra at 300°C, comparing an TMA (a) dose and an HF (b) dose.

a.question: Is all the formed CH3 during the TMA step removed upon HF exposure?
a.answer: All the CH3 seems to be removed as the peaks have similar height.

a.question: Is HF adsorbed on the surface based on the given IR spectra?
a.answer: No, there is no HF formed as there is no positive peak visible in the HF IR active region.

a.question: How many vibrational states of CH3 are there in the given wavenumber range?
a.answer: At least three can be observed. The main peak consists of two sharp peaks close together, with a shoulder to the right.

a.question: Can any peaks be observed between 4000 and 3250 cm^-1 be observed?
a.answer: No, in general there must be a certain signal to noise ratio to be able to identify actual peaks. In this region, the signal seems to be fully comprised of noise.
